seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in CrewAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in CrewAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In CrewAI, Context Window Management issues arise when agents pass increasingly verbose conversation histories, tool outputs, and task contexts between crew members, quickly exhausting LLM token limits. This leads to truncated inputs, forgotten prior decisions, and degraded performance in multi-agent workflows. CrewAI's sequential task delegation exacerbates the problem as each agent appends its full reasoning and outputs without trimming.

howItManifests.scenario: Long-running research crew with sequential delegation
howItManifests.symptom: Agent conversation_history exceeds 100k tokens after 5-6 handoffs
howItManifests.impact: LLM returns 'context_length_exceeded' errors or incoherent responses

howItManifests.scenario: Code review crew with tool-heavy agents
howItManifests.symptom: Tool outputs (code diffs, logs) accumulate without summarization
howItManifests.impact: Later agents receive truncated inputs and hallucinate fixes

howItManifests.symptom: Each agent prepends full chat history to their response
howItManifests.impact: Senior agents lose context of original customer issue

howItManifests.symptom: Task descriptions grow exponentially with each replan
howItManifests.impact: Final execution agent misinterprets drifted objectives

detection.name: Token Count Monitoring
detection.description: Track input token counts per agent task and flag when approaching 80% of model limit

detection.name: Context Drift Detection
detection.description: Compare semantic similarity between original task and accumulated context
detection.code:
from crewai import Task
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer, util
import numpy as np

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

class ContextDriftDetector:
    def __init__(self, threshold=0.7):
        self.threshold = threshold
    
    def detect_drift(self, original_task: Task, current_context: str):
        original_emb = model.encode(original_task.description)
        context_emb = model.encode(current_context[-2000:])  # Last 2000 chars
        similarity = util.cos_sim(original_emb, context_emb).item()
        
        print(f'Context drift: {similarity:.3f} (threshold: {self.threshold})')
        return similarity < self.threshold
    
# Usage
detector = ContextDriftDetector()

# In task callback
if detector.detect_drift(task, task.context.get('history', '')):
    print('🚨 Context drift detected - task objective lost')
    task.context['needs_summary'] = True

detection.name: Loop Detection in Delegation
detection.description: Track agent delegation patterns to detect context-bloated loops

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Summarization
fixes.description: Automatically summarize conversation history before each agent handoff

fixes.name: Token-Budgeted Outputs
fixes.description: Enforce strict token limits on agent outputs to prevent context explosion
fixes.code:
from crewai import Agent
import tiktoken

encoding = tiktoken.get_encoding('cl100k_base')

class TokenBudgetAgent(Agent):
    def __init__(self, *args, max_output_tokens=2000, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        self.max_output_tokens = max_output_tokens
    
    def _generate_response(self, prompt):
        response = super()._generate_response(prompt)
        tokens = encoding.encode(response)
        
        if len(tokens) > self.max_output_tokens:
            # Truncate and add summary
            truncated = encoding.decode(tokens[:self.max_output_tokens-100])
            summary_prompt = f'Summarize this truncated response (max 100 tokens): {truncated}'
            summary = self.llm(summary_prompt)
            response = f'{truncated}\n...[truncated - see summary]\n{summary}'
        
        print(f'📏 Output: {len(encoding.encode(response))} tokens')
        return response

# Usage
researcher = TokenBudgetAgent(
    role='Researcher',
    goal='Research efficiently',
    max_output_tokens=1500,  # Conservative budget
    verbose=True
)

fixes.name: Hierarchical Context Storage
fixes.description: Store context in layered summaries instead of full history

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a callback in CrewAI tasks, automatically monitoring token counts, detecting delegation loops, and context drift across your entire crew execution. When issues are detected, Vex can trigger automatic summarization, context truncation, or even agent replanning without manual intervention.

The Vex SDK provides production-grade observability with real-time dashboards showing context growth patterns per agent role, delegation chains, and token budgets. Remediation policies can be configured declaratively - for example, 'auto-summarize when tokens > 70% limit' or 'fail-fast on detected loops.'

Vex captures full execution traces including intermediate agent states, making root-cause analysis trivial even in complex multi-agent orchestrations.
vexAutomation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex_sdk import Vex

# Install: pip install crewai vex-sdk
vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-key')

# Define context window reliability policy
@vex.policy(
    name='crewai-context-window',
    metrics=['token_usage', 'context_drift', 'delegation_loops'],
    remediation='auto_summarize',
    threshold={'tokens': 0.8}  # 80% of model limit
)
def monitor_context_window(task: Task, agent: Agent, context: dict):
    '''Vex automatically extracts metrics from CrewAI execution'''
    pass

# Agents with Vex integration
researcher = Agent(
    role='Researcher',
    goal='Research without context overflow',
    callbacks=[monitor_context_window],
    verbose=True
)

# Tasks automatically monitored
task1 = Task(
    description='Research topic',
    agent=researcher,
    context={'history': []}  # Vex tracks growth
)

crew = Crew(agents=[researcher], tasks=[task1])

# Vex wraps execution
with vex.span('crew-execution'):
    result = crew.kickoff()

# View issues in Vex dashboard
print('Check Vex dashboard for context issues:', vex.dashboard_url)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `pip install vex-sdk` and one line of code to make your CrewAI agents context-window proof.
